items.builder_takeaway: A browser-based collection of tiny 8-bit computer emulators built with WebAssembly. A neat technical demo but a hobby curiosity with no direct applicability to solo software business decisions.

items.title: Tiny Emulators

items.builder_takeaway: A self-study physics curriculum guide. Educational content unrelated to software building or business decisions.
source_facts: So you want to learn physics (second edition, 2021)

items.builder_takeaway: A speculative Ask HN thread proposing a UI flag for AI-generated articles on Hacker News itself. Meta-discussion with no reproducible outcome or actionable decision.
source_facts: An Ask HN poster proposes adding a flag to mark AI-generated articles.
items.title: Ask HN: Add flag for AI-generated articles
items.uncertainty: Unclear if HN will act on this; purely a discussion thread.

items.builder_takeaway: A personal blog post walking through a first PCB design and assembly project. Hardware hobbyist content, tangential to solo software building.

items.title: Designing and assembling my first PCB

items.builder_takeaway: A personal productivity blog post on reading habits. Lifestyle content, not builder-relevant.
items.title: How to read more books

items.builder_takeaway: A technical writeup on fixing a long-standing compiler issue (in the Gleam language) using Rust arena allocation. Solid engineering content but narrow in scope to Rust/compiler internals, outside a Node/TS/Postgres solo dev stack.
source_facts: A developer closed a three-year-old bug in a Rust-based compiler project by switching to arena allocation.
items.title: Closing a three-year-old issue using Rust arenas

items.builder_takeaway: Concrete, reproducible optimization technique worth knowing if ever profiling hot loops.
source_facts: Adding a logically redundant if-branch allowed the compiler to generate code that runs roughly 4x faster.
items.title: Quadrupling code performance with a "useless" if
items.uncertainty: Applicability depends heavily on language/compiler; may not transfer to typical Node/TS workloads.

items.builder_takeaway: Emerging VCS tooling worth tracking for future workflow evaluation.
source_facts: A community member published a hands-on tutorial for the Jujutsu (jj) version control system.
items.title: Evan's Jujutsu Tutorial
items.uncertainty: Jujutsu adoption is still early; unclear if it offers enough advantage over git to justify switching.

items.builder_takeaway: Potentially useful if doing Mac/SwiftUI work, but narrow personal-blog scope.
source_facts: A developer updated their guide on building Mac-native-feeling apps with SwiftUI after WWDC 2027 changes.
items.title: A WWDC 27 Update on Building a Mac-assed App with SwiftUI
items.uncertainty: Relevance depends on whether current iOS work touches SwiftUI/macOS specifically rather than UIKit/iOS-only.

items.builder_takeaway: An Emacs package embedding the libghostty terminal emulator. Very niche tooling for Emacs users specifically.
source_facts: A developer released ghostel.el, an Emacs terminal emulator built on libghostty.
items.title: ghostel.el - Terminal emulator powered by libghostty

items.builder_takeaway: An editorial/analysis piece critiquing AI-assisted software development. Opinion content without reproducible evidence or a concrete decision to act on.
source_facts: An essay offers a critical analysis of AI-assisted software development practices.
items.title: Know thine enemy: A critical engagement with AI-assisted software development
items.uncertainty: Opinion piece; no reproducible data or benchmarks backing its claims.

items.builder_takeaway: Simon Willison reflects on the 'Directly Responsible Individual' concept and argues LLM agents should never hold that role since accountability requires a human. Thoughtful but a philosophical essay, not an actionable decision.
source_facts: Simon Willison argues that LLM-powered agents should never be considered the Directly Responsible Individual for a project.
items.title: Directly Responsible Individuals (DRI)
items.uncertainty: Opinion/framing piece, not a policy change or tool release.

items.builder_takeaway: Real feature additions to a scraping/screenshot automation tool relevant to data-scraping workflows.
source_facts: shot-scraper 1.11 adds a --js-file option across commands and improves server-start polling for shot-scraper video/multi.
items.title: shot-scraper 1.11

items.builder_takeaway: Directly affects usage-limit planning for anyone on a Claude Max/paid plan using Fable 5.
source_facts: Anthropic extended Claude Fable 5 availability on paid Claude plans, with Claude Code rate limits staying 50% higher, through July 19.
items.title: Anthropic extends Claude Fable 5 access window through July 19
items.uncertainty: Access window could be extended again or could lapse on July 19; sourced via social post relayed through a blog, not an official Anthropic announcement page.

items.builder_takeaway: Real bug fix that prevents a silent-data-loss failure mode for foreign-key-referenced tables.
source_facts: sqlite-utils 4.1.1 fixes a bug where table.transform() could silently delete or modify rows referenced by foreign keys during a transaction.
items.title: sqlite-utils 4.1.1
